widgets_values: What are the next 3 steps in this sequence? 0,1,2,6,16,44,120,...
nodes.title: 2 - Entropy-Bound Sampler

widgets_values:
# ComfyUI-DiffusionGemma — P3 instrumentation example

**Flow:** Loader -> Sampler -> [Preview Text, Preview CanvasState, Preview Flipbook Frames], and Sampler.canvas_trace -> Trace -> [Preview Heatmap, Preview Trace Summary].

DiffusionGemma is a *uniform-state discrete-diffusion* text model (no MASK sentinel, no sigma schedule, ADR-CDG-001/004). It starts from a canvas of random tokens and iteratively commits low-entropy positions under a per-step entropy budget, re-noising the rest, until the canvas stabilizes into text. Finished canvases feed the KV cache for the next block (block-autoregressive).

**Honest positioning:** this pack's value is INSTRUMENTABILITY — a watchable commit-front, an entropy field, a live per-step view, and this Trace node's heatmap/curve — NOT throughput. DiffusionGemma has never been observed to run faster than its own autoregressive Gemma base on hardware where the AR base fits in VRAM: the KV-cache asymmetry is structural (AR pays for canvas width once per token, cached; diffusion pays full-canvas-width cost every step, uncached). Don't come here for speed — come here to watch and steer the trajectory. (repo loose-ends.md, "DiffusionGemma <= its AR base")

Per-node notes below name what each widget/output does and cite the issue that grounds it. Full detail: README.md, plan.md, loose-ends.md, decisions/ in the repo.
nodes.title: Overview — start here

widgets_values:
### 1 — DGemmaLoader (load, don't drive)

Loads model + processor via transformers' `from_pretrained` (ADR-CDG-004: load via transformers, drive via the diffusers pipeline downstream in the Sampler).

- **repo_id** — HF Hub slug (`google/diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it`) or a local path. First load auto-downloads + caches ~11 safetensors shards (~54GB bf16, ungated, Apache-2.0).
- **quant** — `none` is the ONLY option now. bitsandbytes cannot quantize DiffusionGemma's fused 3D MoE expert tensors (it only swaps `nn.Linear`/`Conv1D`, silently skipping ~22.84B of 26B params) — `nf4`/`int8` were removed from the selector rather than left in to silently not do what they claim (issue #18, wall grounded in #4).
- **local_files_only** — off (default): allow HF Hub download + cache. On: force both `from_pretrained` calls to resolve only from the local cache — no network. Useful once the checkpoint is already cached.

A 48GB-class GPU runs `quant=none` with CPU spill (~2.3s/step observed). Smaller cards have no working quantized path yet (#4, AWQ-INT4 is the lead candidate).
nodes.title: Node 1 notes

widgets_values:
### 2 — DGemmaSampler knobs (entropy-bound decoder)

Widget names match `run_diffusion`'s kwargs 1:1 — no separate node-facing vocabulary.

- **num_inference_steps** — the step budget / anneal denominator `S`. Temperature schedule is linear over it: `t = t_min + (t_max-t_min)*(cur_step/S)`.
- **t_min / t_max** — hot->cool temperature schedule across the anneal (default 0.8->0.4). Governs RENOISE COVERAGE (how many positions get re-noised each step), NOT token identity — output is always `argmax`, which is temperature-invariant. Visible "twisting" happens because a position's committed-neighbor context changes and it re-argmaxes.
- **entropy_bound** — a FIXED per-step acceptance budget (not scheduled): how much of the canvas is allowed to commit each step.
- **confidence** — early-stop threshold on mean canvas entropy. DISCONTINUOUS, not a smooth quality dial (issue #10): a byte-identical dead zone was measured across 0.005-0.1, with a cliff at 0.001 — block-autoregression means a threshold change does nothing until it flips one canvas's exit, then all downstream canvases diverge completely.
- **gen_length** — total generation length; tiles into 256-token canvases (blocks), each finished block feeding the KV cache for the next.
- **thinking** — injects the model's `<|think|>` control token. Caveat (issue #9): can consume the ENTIRE canvas thinking and return an empty answer — check `CanvasState.turn_closed` / `answer_tokens`, not just `converged`.
- **seed** — RNG seed for canvas init; best-effort, not bit-exact across library versions (repo loose-ends.md, "seeding/renoise").
nodes.title: Node 2 notes

widgets_values:
### Outputs — what each Preview shows

- **Preview Text** — the `STRING` result. Clean by construction: the model's thought-channel span is excised at the token-id level before this socket, never leaked (even when `thinking=true`).
- **Preview CanvasState** — the validity readout, not just a bare string (ADR-CDG-001 Addendum: a payload can't lie about whether the canvas actually finished). Key fields: `converged`, `committed_fraction`, `steps_used`, `turn_closed` (did the model actually end its turn vs. run out of canvas), `answer_tokens` (pre-EOS count, excludes trailing canvas-fill).
- **Preview Flipbook Frames** — the Sampler's 4th output, `frames`: one decoded STRING per captured diffusion step, in order (`OUTPUT_IS_LIST` — ComfyUI fans this Preview out per frame). RAW decode (skip_special_tokens only, no eos-trim, no thought-channel excision) — early frames are mostly noise, and watching that noise resolve into `text` step by step IS the point (the in-graph version of `tools/flipbook/flipbook.py`'s scrubber). Wire `frames` into any list-consuming node the same way.
- **Preview Trace Summary** (from DGemmaTrace) — the per-step `committed_fraction` ramp (avalanche curve) plus a mask-token corroboration verdict (empirically confirms no absorbing-MASK sentinel, ADR-CDG-001/004).
- **Preview Heatmap** (from DGemmaTrace) — the commit-front image: steps x canvas-positions, nearest-neighbor upscaled by the Trace node's `cell_px` widget (default 6 — a raw map is unreadably small at 1:1).
nodes.title: Outputs notes
